mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which compresses earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral in >60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero predictive power when GDP and retail sales diverge, or forward returns show no statistical difference from baseline across 3 rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a post-shock recovery environment signals supply chain normalization and capacity restoration, which reduces inflationary pressures and supports risk asset valuations over a short horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production rises while equity returns remain negative for >50% of observations, indicating the signal fails to capture supply-side valuation drivers.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing below 1,000 thousand units signals severe demand contraction in the residential sector, which historically precedes broader economic downturns and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances despite housing collapses, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high federal funds rates and rising unemployment indicate stagflationary pressure, which compresses equity multiples by increasing both discount rates and earnings risk, driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when rates and unemployment rise concurrently, or forward returns show no significant difference from baseline across 3 rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth consistently coincides with negative or flat equity returns, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation exceeding 300 on the index scale during a monetary tightening cycle forces aggressive rate hikes, which increases discount rates and equity risk premiums, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >50% of triggered instances despite high inflation and rising rates, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.
